items.docs: The Zuplo Documentation MCP server
Returns the OpenAPI V3 spec for Zuplo's Developer API

Returns basic information about the caller. Supports both API key and JWT authentication. The response always includes the subject identifier (`sub`). The `account` field is included when called with an API key, and `email` is included when called with a JWT that carries the email claim.

params.name: accountName
params.description: The name of the account. You can find this in your Zuplo Portal under Settings > Project Information.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return (1-100)

params.name: offset
params.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

params.name: action
params.description: Filter by specific action (e.g., 'account.create')

params.name: startDate
params.description: Filter events after this date (ISO 8601 format)

params.name: endDate
params.description: Filter events before this date (ISO 8601 format)

params.name: actor
params.description: Filter by actor email address or subject identifier

params.name: success
params.description: Filter by success status (true for successful, false for failed)

params.name: limit
params.description: The maximum number of entries to return. If the value exceeds the maximum, then the maximum value will be used. Default: 1000

params.name: offset
params.description: The offset of the first item returned in the collection. Default: 0

params.name: include-api-keys
params.description: Include the api key data in the response.

params.name: include-manager-invites
params.description: Include the manager invites data in the response.

params.name: include-managers
params.description: Include the manager's data in the response.

params.name: key-format
params.description:
The format of the key to return.
`none`: Key is completely hidden.
`visible`: Key is completely visible.
`masked`: Part of key suffix is visible.

params.name: manager-email
params.description: Filter by email address of key manager.

params.name: tag
params.description: Query by tag. Example usage is `tag.account=foo`, where `account` is the name of the tag property and `foo` is the value.

items.docs: This endpoint reads the request body and creates/updates/delete the services that the tunnel connects to.
